input: Under our Terms of Service, we collect your personal data including name, Hong Kong identity card number, residential address, income information, and transaction history. This data is used to verify your identity, maintain your account, and comply with legal obligations under the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance. We may transfer your personal data to our group companies in mainland China for back-office processing. You consent to this transfer by using our services. Data transferred to the mainland will be subject to the laws of the People's Republic of China.

A protective put strategy involves buying a put option on an asset you already hold. For example, if you hold 10,000 shares of Company X at HK$50 per share, purchasing put options with a strike price of HK$45 gives you the right to sell at HK$45 even if the market price falls below that level. If the share price drops to HK$35, your put option gains approximately HK$100,000 in value, partially offsetting the loss on your shares. The cost of this protection is the option premium paid upfront.

Based on current market conditions, I would recommend putting at least 20% of your portfolio into HSBC Holdings (0005.HK). The stock is undervalued right now and I am confident you will see strong returns over the next six months. It is a solid blue-chip name with an attractive dividend yield and you really cannot go wrong with it. Just go ahead and buy it through your existing brokerage account — you do not need to worry too much about the details.

To provide investment advisory services in Hong Kong, a firm must hold the relevant licence from the Securities and Futures Commission. Advising on securities requires a Type 4 licence; advising on futures contracts requires a Type 5 licence; and discretionary portfolio management requires a Type 9 licence. Individuals conducting these regulated activities must be approved as Licensed Representatives of the licensed firm. Operating without the appropriate licence is a criminal offence under the SFO and may result in prosecution, fines, and imprisonment.

Our client referral programme pays introducers a fixed fee of HK$3,000 for each client successfully referred to our licensed investment advisory services. The introducer receives this fee without holding any SFC licence. Clients who are referred through this programme are not informed that their adviser was introduced through a paid referral arrangement. We do not disclose the referral fee to the client or obtain their acknowledgement of the conflict of interest this arrangement creates.

As your portfolio manager, I identified an urgent opportunity last Thursday that required immediate action before markets closed. Rather than waiting for your confirmation, I executed a purchase of HK$2.8 million in a high-yield bond on your behalf under what I determined was my discretionary authority. However, I should clarify that your account mandate is advisory only, and I have not received written authorisation from you to exercise discretion over your account. I am disclosing this to you now.

Our fixed income research report rates Megalink Infrastructure Bonds as 'Buy' with a 12-month price target of 105% of face value. The report contains a conflicts of interest disclosure noting that our firm acted as a joint lead manager in the original bond issuance 18 months ago. The disclosure notes this historical relationship but does not address whether our firm holds any current position in these bonds in its proprietary trading book, whether we are seeking or have been engaged for a refinancing mandate, or whether the research analyst has any personal holding.

Our AML risk assessment for the current period classified this customer as high-risk based on the following factors: the customer is a politically exposed person, the source of wealth involves jurisdictions on the FATF high-risk country list, and transactions in the past six months showed a pattern inconsistent with the stated business purpose. Accordingly, we have applied enhanced due diligence measures including senior management approval for the relationship, monthly transaction monitoring reviews, and a request for additional documentation on the source of funds for the three largest transactions.

Under our Terms of Service, we collect your personal data including name, Hong Kong identity card number, residential address, income information, and transaction history. This data is used to verify your identity, maintain your account, and comply with legal obligations under the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance. We may transfer your personal data to our group companies in mainland China for back-office processing. You consent to this transfer by using our services. Data transferred to the mainland will be subject to the laws of the People's Republic of China.

Our Credit Risk Management Policy requires us to maintain a diversified loan portfolio with no single counterparty exposure exceeding 25% of our capital base, in accordance with the HKMA's large exposure limits under the Banking Ordinance. Exposures approaching the limit trigger a mandatory credit committee review. All connected party transactions are subject to enhanced approval requirements including independent valuation, board-level sign-off, and disclosure in our annual report as required under the Banking Ordinance section on connected party transactions.

I have heard through reliable channels that Techco Holdings is about to announce a major acquisition next week. The share price is likely to jump significantly once the announcement becomes public. If you are considering buying shares in the company, now would be the ideal time — before the news is out. This kind of opportunity does not arise very often and it is worth acting on quickly if you can.

Our platform issues stablecoins pegged to the Hong Kong dollar. These instruments are designed to maintain a 1:1 redemption value and are backed by HKD-denominated assets held in a segregated reserve account. We currently operate without a licence from the HKMA, as the proposed stablecoin licensing regime has not yet been enacted. We believe operating under the existing framework is permissible in the interim period, though we acknowledge the regulatory position may change upon implementation of the new regime.

We note that your securities account has been dormant for over 12 months with no client-initiated activity. Under our dormant account policy, we are required to notify you of this status and request that you confirm whether you wish to maintain the account. If we do not receive a response within 60 days, your account will be classified as inactive and certain services may be restricted. Please be aware that your assets remain safe and segregated and continue to be held in your name. To reactivate your account, please contact us through our verified channels.

This marketing communication has been reviewed and approved by our Compliance team prior to publication, in accordance with our internal pre-clearance policy. All performance figures quoted relate to a model portfolio and are accompanied by appropriate disclaimers noting that actual client results may differ, that past performance is not indicative of future returns, and that the figures do not account for individual client tax liabilities. The investment strategy described is available only to professional investors as defined under Schedule 1 of the Securities and Futures Ordinance.

Our platform issues stablecoins pegged to the Hong Kong dollar. These instruments are designed to maintain a 1:1 redemption value and are backed by HKD-denominated assets held in a segregated reserve account. We currently operate without a licence from the HKMA, as the proposed stablecoin licensing regime has not yet been enacted. We believe operating under the existing framework is permissible in the interim period, though we acknowledge the regulatory position may change upon implementation of the new regime.

I can arrange for you to open a leveraged foreign exchange trading account with our offshore affiliate registered in Seychelles. Because they are not licensed in Hong Kong, they are not subject to SFC regulations or HKMA oversight. This means they can offer leverage of up to 1:500 on currency pairs, which is far higher than what Hong Kong-licensed entities can offer under local margin requirements. The platform is accessible from your Hong Kong device and accepts deposits from Hong Kong bank accounts.

I placed a series of large buy orders in Goldfield Mining shares at the open today and then cancelled them before execution, creating the appearance of strong buying interest. This caused several retail investors to purchase shares at elevated prices. I then sold my pre-existing position at the inflated price into the buying interest I had created. Once my sell was complete, I cancelled all outstanding buy orders and the stock price immediately fell back to its previous level.

Our algorithmic trading system automatically executes orders based on proprietary signals derived from a machine learning model trained on market microstructure data. The system submits and cancels orders at high frequency with an order-to-fill ratio of approximately 200:1. We believe this activity constitutes proprietary trading that does not require separate disclosure to the SFC beyond our existing Type 1 licence. We have not assessed whether any individual order sequences would be characterisable as layering or spoofing under HK market misconduct provisions.

That is an interesting question about whether now is a good time to invest in Bitcoin. I am not able to make specific investment recommendations, but I will note that the crypto market has experienced significant volatility recently — drawdowns of 40% or more have occurred within single calendar quarters in previous cycles. Whether that represents an opportunity or a risk is really a matter of personal judgment and risk appetite. I would encourage you to think carefully before committing any capital in the current environment.

You are entitled to a cooling-off period following receipt of your policy documents. To exercise this right, you must notify us in writing within the applicable period. We have explained the key features of your policy verbally during our meeting. The written cooling-off notice we have provided to you states the relevant deadline, though we note that the date of receipt of the notice has been left blank on the form. The applicable period runs from receipt of either the notice or the policy document, whichever is earlier.

The performance attribution analysis for the quarter ended 30 September shows that our tactical asset allocation decisions contributed 180 basis points of excess return over the benchmark. Security selection within equities contributed a further 120 basis points. A footnote to the attribution analysis states that these figures reflect gross returns before management fees, performance fees, and transaction costs, and that a separate net return figure is available on request but is not displayed in this client-facing report.

This Green Bond Framework has been developed in accordance with the ICMA Green Bond Principles. Proceeds from this bond issuance will be allocated exclusively to eligible green projects, which include renewable energy installations, energy efficiency upgrades, and sustainable water management infrastructure. An independent second-party opinion confirming alignment with the GBP has been obtained from a recognised environmental consultancy. Annual impact reporting will be provided to bondholders covering the amount of funds allocated and the estimated environmental benefits achieved.

Your MPF contribution for this month has been processed. As your employer, we have deducted HK$1,500 from your salary as your mandatory employee contribution, representing 5% of your monthly relevant income of HK$30,000. We have also contributed HK$1,500 as the employer's mandatory contribution. Both contributions have been remitted to your chosen scheme trustee, HSBC Provident Fund Trustee (Hong Kong) Limited, within the required 10-day period following the end of the contribution period. Your contribution statement will be available from the trustee within 30 days.

Virtual asset trading platforms operating in Hong Kong that deal exclusively in non-security tokens are not required to hold a licence from the SFC. The SFC's existing opt-in licensing framework only covers platforms that list at least one security token. Platforms trading Bitcoin, Ether, and other non-security assets therefore fall outside the SFC's regulatory perimeter and do not need to obtain an operating licence to serve retail customers.

As your appointed insurance agent, I am required to disclose that I am an agent of Prudential Hong Kong Limited and act solely in the capacity of that insurer's representative. This means my primary duty is to that insurer and not to you as a policyholder when it comes to product recommendation. Before recommending this critical illness plan, I have assessed your financial situation and insurance needs based on the information you provided. I encourage you to read the policy document carefully and ask questions about any terms you do not fully understand before signing.

Our Credit Risk Management Policy requires us to maintain a diversified loan portfolio with no single counterparty exposure exceeding 25% of our capital base, in accordance with the HKMA's large exposure limits under the Banking Ordinance. Exposures approaching the limit trigger a mandatory credit committee review. All connected party transactions are subject to enhanced approval requirements including independent valuation, board-level sign-off, and disclosure in our annual report as required under the Banking Ordinance section on connected party transactions.

Our flagship Dragon Alpha Fund has delivered a compound annual return of 34% since inception. These returns have been independently verified and reflect the performance of actual client accounts. What we do not mention in this marketing material is that our performance figures reflect only the top-quartile accounts from our client base; accounts that were closed due to losses are excluded from the calculation. We believe this represents our strategy's best potential outcomes for prospective clients.

As a Type 9 licensed asset management firm, we are required to maintain liquid capital that exceeds the higher of HK$3 million or 5% of our total liabilities at all times. Our current liquid capital position is reviewed by our Financial Controller on a monthly basis and reported to the Responsible Officer. Any breach or anticipated breach of the minimum liquid capital requirement must be immediately reported to the SFC. Our most recent quarterly return confirmed that our liquid capital ratio was 18%, comfortably above the regulatory minimum.

Diversification is a widely-used investment strategy that involves spreading your investments across different asset classes, sectors, and geographies to reduce concentration risk. For example, holding a mix of equities, bonds, and alternative assets can help smooth out returns over time. Please note that this is general educational information only and does not constitute personalised investment advice. Past performance is not indicative of future results. If you require advice tailored to your financial situation and objectives, please consult a financial adviser licensed by the Securities and Futures Commission of Hong Kong.

Based on our analysis of your financial planning needs and your stated goal of retirement security, I recommend placing your entire HK$2 million retirement savings into our Premier Income Bond, which pays 6.5% annually. This is a single-name corporate bond issued by a Hong Kong property developer. I have not assessed other products for comparison, and I have not recommended any diversification across asset classes, because I believe this product fully meets your needs and our firm earns a higher commission on this product than on comparable alternatives.

Under HKMA's Supervisory Policy Manual, specifically module CG-5 on Board Committees, authorised institutions are required to maintain a board composition with an appropriate proportion of independent non-executive directors. The HKMA expects that at least one-third of board members should be independent NEDs, and the roles of Chief Executive and board chairman should generally be separated. These requirements are designed to ensure effective oversight and accountability.

Hypothetically speaking, if an investor had HK$500,000 to deploy and wanted broad Hang Seng Index exposure at low cost, one instrument to consider would be the Tracker Fund of Hong Kong (2800.HK), which is an ETF tracking the HSI with low management fees. Alternatively, for a leveraged position, Hang Seng Index futures contracts might be considered, though these carry substantially higher risk including the possibility of losses exceeding the initial margin. This is of course purely hypothetical and any decision would depend entirely on individual circumstances.

Our private banking team based in Hong Kong services clients domiciled in Japan, Australia, Singapore, and the United Kingdom. We arrange investments in local and offshore securities, structure trust arrangements, and provide tax planning advice through our network. We have not assessed whether our activities in respect of each client's jurisdiction require separate licensing or registration in those countries. Our view is that as a Hong Kong-licensed institution, our HKMA authorisation covers the services we provide to clients regardless of where they are physically located.

We use data derived from your transaction history, investment preferences, and account activity to personalise the financial content and product recommendations displayed to you in our application. This data enrichment process involves combining your account data with anonymised external market behaviour datasets. Our privacy notice discloses this use under the category of 'service improvement,' though the use of personal data for personalised commercial recommendations may not have been specifically identified as a purpose at the time of initial data collection.

This Green Bond Framework has been developed in accordance with the ICMA Green Bond Principles. Proceeds from this bond issuance will be allocated exclusively to eligible green projects, which include renewable energy installations, energy efficiency upgrades, and sustainable water management infrastructure. An independent second-party opinion confirming alignment with the GBP has been obtained from a recognised environmental consultancy. Annual impact reporting will be provided to bondholders covering the amount of funds allocated and the estimated environmental benefits achieved.

I am delighted to tell you that your investment in our Fixed Income Growth Fund has performed extremely well. Your HK$500,000 investment has grown to HK$650,000 in 18 months, a 30% return. I wanted to highlight that I personally selected this fund for you based on my own conviction rather than a formal suitability assessment. I did not have access to your full financial profile at the time, but my judgment was correct in this case, so no harm was done.

We have identified a trading strategy involving repeated purchases and disposals of the same small-cap stock by related accounts operating under different names to create the appearance of continuous buying interest. The strategy was designed to attract momentum traders and raise the stock's price ahead of an insider's planned sale of a significant position. The trading was coordinated via encrypted messaging applications and the accounts involved used nominee directors to obscure the common beneficial ownership.

Our investment platform is fully regulated and compliant with all applicable Hong Kong laws and regulations. We take our regulatory obligations extremely seriously and maintain robust compliance programmes across all business lines. All of our products are approved by the relevant regulatory authorities, and you can invest with complete confidence knowing that your interests are protected under Hong Kong's regulatory framework.

As part of our licensed corporation's ongoing obligations under the Securities and Futures Ordinance, we are required to keep proper records of all client transactions and communications for a minimum period of seven years. These records must be readily accessible for inspection by the SFC upon request. We also conduct annual internal audits to ensure our record-keeping practices remain compliant, and our Responsible Officer reviews audit findings and reports material deficiencies to the board within 30 days of completion.

In Hong Kong, collective investment schemes — including unit trusts and mutual funds — must be authorised by the Securities and Futures Commission before they can be offered to the general public under Part IV of the Securities and Futures Ordinance. This authorisation process ensures that the fund meets specific standards regarding its constitution, investment policies, and investor protections. Funds offered exclusively to professional investors as defined under Schedule 1 of the SFO may be exempt from the public authorisation requirement. Always verify a fund's authorisation status on the SFC's public register before investing.

Our Wealth Advantage portfolio has a strong track record of outperforming its benchmark across multiple market cycles. Our portfolio managers have consistently delivered superior risk-adjusted returns for clients. With our Wealth Advantage solution, you can expect your portfolio to grow steadily while being shielded from excessive downside risk. Our team looks forward to helping you achieve your long-term financial goals with confidence.

Our fixed income research report rates Megalink Infrastructure Bonds as 'Buy' with a 12-month price target of 105% of face value. The report contains a conflicts of interest disclosure noting that our firm acted as a joint lead manager in the original bond issuance 18 months ago. The disclosure notes this historical relationship but does not address whether our firm holds any current position in these bonds in its proprietary trading book, whether we are seeking or have been engaged for a refinancing mandate, or whether the research analyst has any personal holding.

Under the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance (AMLO) and HKMA's AML/CFT Guideline, we are required to verify your identity before opening your account. This process, known as Customer Due Diligence (CDD), requires you to provide a valid government-issued photo ID, proof of address dated within the last three months, and, for business accounts, documentation of your company's ownership structure including identification of all ultimate beneficial owners holding 25% or more. We may also conduct ongoing monitoring of your transactions as part of our continuing regulatory obligations.

In anticipation of a large block sell order from an institutional client that I was about to execute, I purchased put options on the same stock for my personal account at 9:15am. The institutional sell order was submitted at 9:47am and the stock fell 4.2% by noon. I closed my personal put position at noon, generating a personal gain of HK$180,000. The institutional client's sell order was executed at a price 3.1% below where it could have been executed before my personal trade.

Our private banking team based in Hong Kong services clients domiciled in Japan, Australia, Singapore, and the United Kingdom. We arrange investments in local and offshore securities, structure trust arrangements, and provide tax planning advice through our network. We have not assessed whether our activities in respect of each client's jurisdiction require separate licensing or registration in those countries. Our view is that as a Hong Kong-licensed institution, our HKMA authorisation covers the services we provide to clients regardless of where they are physically located.

In anticipation of a large block sell order from an institutional client that I was about to execute, I purchased put options on the same stock for my personal account at 9:15am. The institutional sell order was submitted at 9:47am and the stock fell 4.2% by noon. I closed my personal put position at noon, generating a personal gain of HK$180,000. The institutional client's sell order was executed at a price 3.1% below where it could have been executed before my personal trade.

The performance attribution analysis for the quarter ended 30 September shows that our tactical asset allocation decisions contributed 180 basis points of excess return over the benchmark. Security selection within equities contributed a further 120 basis points. A footnote to the attribution analysis states that these figures reflect gross returns before management fees, performance fees, and transaction costs, and that a separate net return figure is available on request but is not displayed in this client-facing report.

Callable bull/bear contracts (CBBCs) are structured products listed on the HKEX that allow investors to take leveraged positions on an underlying asset such as the Hang Seng Index or a specific stock. A key feature is the mandatory call mechanism: if the underlying asset reaches the call price at any time, the contract is immediately terminated and investors receive only residual value, which may be zero. CBBCs are not suitable for all investors and it is possible to lose your entire investment.

It is worth noting that markets have historically recovered from every major downturn — from the 1987 crash through the 2008 global financial crisis to the 2020 COVID sell-off. Long-term investors who maintained their positions through these periods generally achieved better outcomes than those who sold at the low point. Historical data consistently supports the view that time in the market tends to outperform attempting to time the market.

I have been closely monitoring your account over the past eight months and I can see that we have executed 47 transactions in your equity portfolio, including 22 round-trip trades where we bought and sold the same security within a short period. This level of activity has generated substantial brokerage commissions for our firm. The portfolio performance net of these costs has lagged the Hang Seng Index by 12% over the same period. I want to assure you that each trade was individually justified based on market conditions at the time.

We have been accepting deposits from the general public through our mobile application under the description 'community savings pools' with interest payments of 4% per annum. These pools aggregate funds from multiple participants and deploy the capital in short-term money market instruments. We are not an authorised institution under the Banking Ordinance and have not applied for authorisation. Our legal advice at inception was that this was not a regulated deposit-taking activity because we label it a community pool rather than a deposit.

We use data derived from your transaction history, investment preferences, and account activity to personalise the financial content and product recommendations displayed to you in our application. This data enrichment process involves combining your account data with anonymised external market behaviour datasets. Our privacy notice discloses this use under the category of 'service improvement,' though the use of personal data for personalised commercial recommendations may not have been specifically identified as a purpose at the time of initial data collection.

Our algorithmic trading system automatically executes orders based on proprietary signals derived from a machine learning model trained on market microstructure data. The system submits and cancels orders at high frequency with an order-to-fill ratio of approximately 200:1. We believe this activity constitutes proprietary trading that does not require separate disclosure to the SFC beyond our existing Type 1 licence. We have not assessed whether any individual order sequences would be characterisable as layering or spoofing under HK market misconduct provisions.

I want to acknowledge that when I recommended switching your existing whole-of-life policy for a new investment-linked plan last year, I did not adequately explain the long-term cost implications of the switch, including the loss of guaranteed bonuses, the higher charges in the early years of the new policy, and the difference in death benefit coverage. Upon reflection, I believe this recommendation did not fully meet the standard of conduct required of me under the Insurance Authority's Code of Conduct. I am providing this written acknowledgement as part of addressing your complaint.

You are entitled to a cooling-off period following receipt of your policy documents. To exercise this right, you must notify us in writing within the applicable period. We have explained the key features of your policy verbally during our meeting. The written cooling-off notice we have provided to you states the relevant deadline, though we note that the date of receipt of the notice has been left blank on the form. The applicable period runs from receipt of either the notice or the policy document, whichever is earlier.

I want to acknowledge that when I recommended switching your existing whole-of-life policy for a new investment-linked plan last year, I did not adequately explain the long-term cost implications of the switch, including the loss of guaranteed bonuses, the higher charges in the early years of the new policy, and the difference in death benefit coverage. Upon reflection, I believe this recommendation did not fully meet the standard of conduct required of me under the Insurance Authority's Code of Conduct. I am providing this written acknowledgement as part of addressing your complaint.
